questions.question: When leading a team, the first thing you do is...

options.text: Communicate a big, inspiring vision
Recognize the individual strengths of each member
Set clear rules and targets

questions.question: When conflict arises, you...

options.text: Guide towards a solution aligned with the vision
Act as a mediator and listen to all parties
Make a firm decision for the sake of efficiency

questions.question: A team member fails to meet a target. You...

options.text: Remind them of the importance of their contribution to the vision
Guide them to improve their skills
Evaluate and give clear consequences

questions.question: In a meeting, you prefer to...

options.text: Motivate and inspire the team
Ensure everyone has a chance to speak
Get straight to the point and make decisions

questions.question: You are respected because of...

options.text: Your big ideas
Your care for team development
Your firmness and results

questions.question: When facing major change, you...

options.text: See it as an opportunity for a new vision
Ensure everyone is comfortable with the change
Make a detailed and measurable action plan

questions.question: How do you give rewards?

options.text: Public recognition and inspiring stories
Personalized gifts according to their interests
Bonuses or promotions based on metrics

questions.question: Under high pressure, you...

options.text: Stay focused on the big picture
Ensure the team's mental health
Increase intensity and discipline

questions.question: For important decisions, you...

options.text: Involve the team to align with the vision
Listen to everyone's input before deciding
Decide alone with data and logic

questions.question: Your team is demotivated, you...

options.text: Tell success stories and the big vision
Find out the cause and give personal support
Give new deadlines and challenges

questions.question: A new team member needs guidance, you...

options.text: Explain the vision and let them find their own way
Guide them step by step patiently
Give clear SOPs and targets

questions.question: What are you most proud of as a leader?

options.text: The impact and change we made
The individual growth within my team
The targets and results we achieved

questions.question: When a team member has a new idea, you...

options.text: See if it aligns with the vision
Appreciate and encourage them to develop it
Evaluate with data before agreeing

questions.question: You are best suited to lead a team that...

options.text: Needs direction and inspiration
Needs development and support
Needs structure and efficiency

questions.question: The leadership legacy you want to leave behind...

options.text: A big vision that changes the industry
Great people that I helped grow
Systems and results that last

questions.question: When delegating an important task, your approach is...

options.text: Delegate the end result and let them be creative
Choose the person who most needs to learn that thing to grow
Give detailed instructions and a strict deadline

questions.question: When recruiting new team members, you look most for...

options.text: Their passion and belief in the company's mission
Attitude, empathy, and cultural fit with the team
Track record, technical skills, and solid portfolio

questions.question: On Monday morning, you start the week by...

options.text: Giving a short pep talk to fire up the team
Greeting everyone and asking about their weekend
Immediately reviewing weekly targets and dividing tasks

questions.question: If the company has to make budget cuts...

options.text: Use this moment to innovate and pivot strategy
Try hard to protect employee welfare and facilities
Cut inefficiencies firmly and quickly without hesitation

questions.question: A project is behind schedule. You will...

options.text: Remind them again how important this project is for the future
Check if the team is exhausted and help overcome their obstacles
Ask for overtime and overhaul the process to be faster

questions.question: For you, the definition of 'success' in a project is...

options.text: Creating a new breakthrough in the market
A solid, happy team that learns many new things
Achieving or exceeding KPIs and revenue targets

questions.question: When organizing a team building event, you choose...

options.text: A hackathon or vision board session
A relaxing retreat or deep sharing session
Competitive sports or an Escape Room that gets adrenaline pumping

questions.question: Your biggest weakness as a leader might be...

options.text: Often missing small technical details
Too soft and often avoid conflict
Too harsh, demanding, and lacking empathy

questions.question: There is a brilliant team member (High Performer) but with a toxic attitude...

options.text: Try to realign their values with the company's mission
Conduct an in-depth counseling session to find the root of their behavior
Issue a warning or fire them if it ruins team efficiency

questions.question: When someone breaks office rules...

options.text: Evaluate whether the rule is outdated and needs to be changed
Ask them privately why they did it
Enforce sanctions according to the handbook to be fair

questions.question: Dealing with negative rumors or gossip at the office...

options.text: Hold a Town Hall to speak transparently and clarify the vision
Approach the relevant parties personally to ease the atmosphere
Send a firm email reminding about work professionalism

questions.question: If a top employee resigns...

options.text: Remind them of the dreams we haven't achieved together
Ask if there's anything you can do for their future career
Accept it and immediately ask them to prepare handover documents

questions.question: Your office door policy is...

options.text: Open anytime for crazy ideas and innovation
Open anytime for venting or personal issues
Closed when focused, open only for urgent matters

questions.question: The work culture you want to build is...

options.text: Bold, innovative, and boundary-breaking
Inclusive, supportive, and family-like
Competitive, highly disciplined, and results-oriented

questions.question: When a subordinate asks something you don't know...

options.text: Frame it as 'a mystery for us to solve together'
Ask for help and input from the team openly
Ask for a moment to research it and give a definitive answer

questions.question: Your role analogy in the company is like...

options.text: A captain steering the ship to a new continent
A gardener tending plants until they bear fruit
A war general leading troops to victory

questions.question: If a meeting is too quiet, you will...

options.text: Throw out a provocative idea to get them debating enthusiastically
Gently call on each person by name to ask for their opinion
Assume everyone agrees and close the meeting

questions.question: Your view on failure is...

options.text: A stepping stone to greater discovery
A valuable moment to learn and mature the team
A waste of resources that should be avoided as much as possible

questions.question: When an employee is often late...

options.text: Ask if they've lost passion for our vision
Check if they have personal problems at home
Give a warning letter for violating discipline

questions.question: Your strongest communication style is...

options.text: Charismatic large presentations
In-depth one-on-one sessions
Short, concise, and to-the-point emails/memos

questions.question: When two departments blame each other...

options.text: Gather them and remind them of the shared ultimate goal
Act as a facilitator so they can empathize with each other
Clarify the boundaries of SOPs and SLAs between divisions in writing

questions.question: The thing that keeps you up at night is...

options.text: Fear that the company is falling behind innovation trends
Thinking about a team member who might be unhappy/stressed
Anxiety about not meeting the end-of-quarter target

questions.question: When welcoming a big new client...

options.text: Present the dreams and big impact we will achieve together
Build personal relationships and strong trust
Present detailed timelines, cost structures, and metrics

questions.question: When monitoring remote work (Work From Home)...

options.text: Often hold virtual town halls to keep spirits up
More often call to ask how they're doing
Use strict tracker software and daily reports

questions.question: If the team is celebrating a small win (Quick Win)...

options.text: Use the euphoria to challenge them to a bigger goal
Treat them to a casual meal to strengthen bonding
Give brief praise then immediately refocus on the main target

questions.question: When choosing a project management tool...

options.text: Choose a tool with a cool, futuristic interface
Choose the tool easiest for the whole team to learn
Choose a tool with the best analytics and reporting features

questions.question: When you are very stressed as a leader...

options.text: Isolate yourself to imagine a better future vision
Seek a trusted friend or mentor to vent to
Go crazy in work and increase work hours (hustle)

questions.question: You will be very disappointed if a team member is...

options.text: Pessimistic, cynical, and doesn't believe in the shared goal
Selfish, undermines others, and unwilling to share knowledge
Lazy, makes excuses, and doesn't meet KPIs

questions.question: A brilliant idea is rejected by investors/boss...

options.text: Still believe it's a good idea and find another way to realize it
Comfort the disappointed team and reassure them that we tried
Analyze the rejection reasons and revise the numbers/data

questions.question: How do you position yourself in a team photo?

options.text: In the middle or slightly in front as a leadership symbol
On the edge or behind so the team stands out
Anywhere as long as the formation looks neat and symmetrical

questions.question: The ultimate goal of your leadership is...

options.text: To leave a legacy recognized by the world
To produce new leaders who are even greater than me
To dominate the market and be the most profitable

questions.question: When you have to lay someone off...

options.text: Explain that this is a difficult step to save the ship
Give empathy, best severance, and recommend them elsewhere
Do it professionally, quickly, and without too much emotional drama

questions.question: Your favorite books or shows are usually in the genre of...

options.text: Biographies of great figures or predictions of future trends
Psychology, emotional intelligence, or self-improvement
Business strategy, finance, or military tactics

questions.question: A client complains loudly to your team...

options.text: Reassure the client with promises of better quality
Calm the client and ensure the team's mental health isn't down
Immediately find the root of the technical problem and fix it that same day

questions.question: What is the best advice you would give to a young leader?

options.text: Never be afraid to dream too big
Take care of your people, and they will take care of your business
Focus on the numbers and don't let feelings override logic
